emptyState.title: No compensation
emptyState.description: Compensation will appear here once added

pendingChange.summaryLabel: There are multiple pending changes to {{name}}'s compensation.

modal.title: Review pending changes
modal.description: These compensation changes are scheduled to take effect on the dates below. Cancel any change to remove it.

details.newJob: {{name}} will start an additional job as {{title}} at {{formattedRate}}
details.newJobNoTitle: {{name}} will start an additional job at {{formattedRate}}
details.newJobNoRate: {{name}} will start an additional job as {{title}}
details.minWageEnabled: Minimum wage adjustment will be enabled at {{formattedWage}}
details.minWageEnabledNoRate: Minimum wage adjustment will be enabled
details.minWageDisabled: Minimum wage adjustment will be removed
details.minWageChanged: Minimum wage adjustment rate will change to {{formattedWage}}

twoPercentStakeholderLabel: Select if employee is a 2% shareholder
paymentUnitDescription: The period over which the compensation amount is tracked (e.g., hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, annually).
adjustForMinimumWageDescription: Determines whether the compensation should be adjusted for minimum wage. Only applies to Nonexempt employees.
minimumWageDescription: What minimum wage requirement should compensation be adjusted to
stateWcCoveredDescription: Washington administers <wcLink>workers’ compensation insurance</wcLink> to protect workers and employers from the financial impact of a work-related injury. Indicate here if this employee is exempt from the workers’ comp tax.

stateWcCoveredOptions.no: No, this employee is not covered

stateWcClassCodeDescription: The risk class code associated with this employee’s job function. We need this to pay and file your taxes correctly.

federalMinimumPay.label: Commission-only employees must earn the federal minimum pay
federalMinimumPay.body: Federal laws say that employees not eligible for overtime should be paid at least $684 per week ($35,568 per year). You’ve classified this employee as not eligible for overtime—make sure they meet the Department of Labor’s definition of an exempt employee.

minimumWage.label: Commission-only employees must earn at least the minimum wage
minimumWage.body: To stay compliant, <minimumWageLink>check your local regulations</minimumWageLink>.

ownerSalary.label: The IRS requires owners of S corps to pay themselves a reasonable salary similar to others in the same role, before taking any distributions.

validations.jobTitleSentence: Job title is a required field
validations.title: Title is a required field
validations.exemptThreshold: Most employees who make under {{limit}}/year should be eligible for overtime.
validations.classificationChangeNotification: Changing this employee's classification will immediately delete their additional jobs.
validations.classificationChangeRemovesSecondaryJobs: Only employees that are paid by the hour can have multiple jobs.
validations.rateExemptThreshold: FLSA Exempt employees must meet salary threshold of {{limit}}/year
validations.paymentUnit: Payment unit must be one of Hour, Week, Month, or Year
validations.minimumWage: Please select minimum wage for adjustment
validations.stateWcClassCode: Please select a risk class code
validations.effectiveDate: Effective date is a required field
validations.effectiveDateBeforeHire: Effective date cannot be before the employee's hire date.
validations.effectiveDateBeforeMin: Effective date must be in the future
